name: TC-OT-008 — Cache eviction + re-fetch: new token issued_at >= original issued_at
description:
**What this test does:**
Executes the full cache eviction cycle in three steps:
1. GET /token → record Token A (issued_at = TA)
2. DELETE /token/cache → evict the cached token (expect 204)
3. GET /token → record Token B (issued_at = TB)

Asserts that TB >= TA — Token B is at least as fresh as Token A, meaning the eviction caused the service to re-fetch or serve the same still-valid token.

**Why it matters:** This proves the eviction-then-refresh path works end-to-end.
descriptionHtml:
What this test does:
Executes the full cache eviction cycle in three steps:

  1. GET /token → record Token A (issued_at = TA)
  2. DELETE /token/cache → evict the cached token (expect 204)
  3. GET /token → record Token B (issued_at = TB)

Asserts that TB >= TA — Token B is at least as fresh as Token A, meaning the eviction caused the service to re-fetch or serve the same still-valid token.

Why it matters: This proves the eviction-then-refresh path works end-to-end.
